text: Patellofemoral pain is among the most common injuries in recreational runners.
Current evidence does not support alignment, muscle weakness, or patellar maltracking as causes of patellofemoral pain.
Both external loads (training parameter changes, biomechanics) and internal loads (sleep, psychological stress) should be considered when addressing patellofemoral pain causes.
Interventions targeting biomechanical factors (strengthening, gait modification, footwear changes) can reduce patellofemoral pain symptoms even when those factors did not cause the injury.
Management of patellofemoral pain should maintain relative homeostasis between external load and individual capacity.
A graded exposure to load (movement, exercise, and running) is recommended for patellofemoral pain management, adjusted to the individual's capacity including sleep and psychosocial factors.
Psychosocial factors such as stress and sleep quality should be assessed as part of a holistic patellofemoral pain treatment approach in runners.
